grant.summary: Amin Reihani, Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering at Rutgers, has received a five-year, $550,000 National Science Foundation (NSF) CAREER Award for his project on how metal nanoparticles use light to promote chemical reactions, focusing on developing advanced nano-scale thermal measurement tools.
grant.department_or_lab: Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, Rutgers School of Engineering
grant.why_it_matters: This grant indicates a need for specialized lab equipment and consumables for micro- and nano-scale research, including advanced thermal measurement tools and materials for catalysis.

ai_summary: Rutgers Professor Amin Reihani secured a five-year, $550,000 NSF CAREER Award for research on metal nanoparticles, chemical reactions, and advanced nano-scale thermal measurement tools. This significant funding indicates new purchasing power and a sustained need for specialized lab supplies, reagents, and analytical equipment related to nanotechnology, chemistry, and thermal analysis, presenting a strong sales opportunity for your team.
